name: Split World Mastercard
effective_cost: Free — no annual fee, but this is a BNPL card with no rewards program at all

verdict.text: Not a rewards card — every purchase is forced into a 3-month installment plan whether you want that or not

earn_note: Split isn't a rewards card — it's U.S. Bank's buy-now-pay-later product wearing a Mastercard. Every purchase of $100+ is automatically split into 3 interest-free monthly payments; purchases under $100 in a billing cycle are bundled into one Small Purchase Plan and split the same way. You can extend a Plan to 6 or 12 months for a fixed monthly Plan Fee, but the 3-month default can't be turned off — there's no way to just carry a normal revolving balance.

points.note: There is nothing to redeem. The entire value proposition is interest-free installment financing, not earning.

transfer_partners.highlight: No rewards program — nothing to transfer.

insurance.detail: Not responsible for unauthorized transactions once reported
Not publicly documented for this card
Not publicly documented for this card
Not publicly documented for this card

protection_note: No purchase-protection or extended-warranty guide published for this card — it's positioned purely as a payment-plan product, not a benefits card.
rental_note: No auto rental coverage documented publicly for this card.

services.name: 3-month Plans, no fee, no interest
services.detail: Every purchase $100+ is automatically divided into 3 equal monthly payments with no interest and no Plan Fee.

services.name: 6- or 12-month extension
services.detail: Extend any Plan to 6 or 12 months, still interest-free, for a small fixed monthly Plan Fee disclosed at the time of extension.

services.name: Plan management
services.detail: View and extend Plans in the U.S. Bank Mobile App or online banking before the billing cycle in which the Plan was created closes.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Their purchases follow the same automatic 3-month Plan structure

additional_cards.note: No cost to add — but every authorized user's spend gets split into Plans the same way the primary cardholder's does.

timeline.text: U.S. Bank launches the Split World Mastercard, its first card built entirely around automatic installment-plan purchases rather than a traditional rewards or revolving-balance structure.
